text:
```json
{
  "activation_status": "activated",
  "risk_level": "elevated",
  "confidence": 0.74,
  "drivers": [
    "RBI repo rate at 5.25% (paused after 125 bps of cuts from 6.50%), placing the rate cycle in late-cutting / early-pause phase; FD reinvestment yields are structurally compressed relative to the prior cycle peak, supporting the advisor's rationale for migration to a corporate bond fund",
    "Real interest rate derived at ~3.15% (repo 5.25% minus core CPI 2.1%), which is materially above the +2% restrictive threshold; this elevated real rate environment is supportive of high-quality fixed income instruments such as AAA corporate bond funds, as nominal yields remain attractive relative to inflation",
    "10Y G-Sec yield at 6.99% (up 44 bps from 6.55% in November 2025, with an intraday spike to 7.19% on April 3); the yield curve has steepened under crude-oil and geopolitical pressure, which creates mark-to-market duration risk for any bond fund with meaningful tenor exposure — ICICI Prudential Corporate Bond Fund's positioning on the curve requires advisor scrutiny",
    "Brent crude at $118.95/barrel (up ~86% YoY from ~$64), driven by Hormuz disruption blocking ~20% of global oil trade; this is a primary macro stress signal with direct pass-through to Indian CPI (food and fuel), fiscal deficit, and INR — all of which bear on the fixed income outlook",
    "INR at 94.79 vs USD (10.7% depreciation over 12 months from 85.60); forex reserves down $25B to $703B; currency stress of this magnitude elevates imported inflation risk and may constrain RBI's ability to resume cutting, compressing the capital appreciation thesis for duration-sensitive bond funds",
    "FII net equity outflows of Rs 56,401 Cr in April 2026 alone (~$5.97B), the heaviest single-month outflow in the data window; DII absorption only partial (Rs 34,617 Cr, partial data); this risk-off dynamic in equities has spillover implications for broader market sentiment and liquidity conditions",
    "CPI headline at 3.40% (March 2026, rising from 3.21% in February) and IMD forecasting below-normal monsoon at 92% of LPA with 66% probability of below-normal or deficient outcome; food inflation upside risk is material and could push headline CPI toward or above the 4% RBI target in H2 FY27, altering the rate-cut path",
    "GDP growth strong at 7.8% in Q3 FY26 (manufacturing 13.3%, services 9.5%); capacity utilisation at 75.6% (above the 75% late-expansion threshold); PMI composite at 58.3 — the growth backdrop does not argue for emergency rate cuts, reinforcing the pause narrative and limiting near-term bond price appreciation from further rate reductions",
    "Government fiscal consolidation on track: fiscal deficit YTD at 80.38% of RE with -7.0% YoY improvement; capex at Rs 9.29L Cr (+14.46% YoY); FY27 fiscal deficit target 4.3% of GDP with market borrowing of Rs 13.03L Cr — supply of government paper remains elevated, which exerts upward pressure on yields and compresses spread compression potential for corporate bonds",
    "India-US rate differential at 162 bps (5.25% minus 3.625% Fed midpoint); within the 100–200 bps adequate range but not compelling for carry-driven capital inflows; Fed on indefinite hold with leadership transition (Warsh replacing Powell ~May 15) adding policy uncertainty to the external environment"
  ],
  "flags": [
    "crude_oil_shock_active_hormuz_disruption",
    "inr_depreciation_stress_10pct_12m",
    "yield_curve_steepening_duration_risk",
    "monsoon_below_normal_food_inflation_upside",
    "fii_outflow_stress_april_2026",
    "real_rate_highly_restrictive_rate_cut_path_uncertain",
    "private_limited_company_product_eligibility_unresolved_str_007",
    "fed_leadership_transition_external_policy_uncertainty",
    "walr_transmission_data_stale_120_days"
  ],
  "reasoning_paragraph": "The macro environment as of April 2026 presents a structurally supportive but tactically stressed backdrop for the proposed migration of Rs 50 lakh from fixed deposits into ICICI Prudential Corporate Bond Fund. The core advisor rationale — that the late-cutting / early-pause rate cycle compresses FD reinvestment yields, making high-quality corporate bond funds relatively more attractive — is directionally valid: the RBI repo rate has been cut 125 bps to 5.25%, and with real rates at approximately 3.15% (repo minus core CPI of 2.1%), nominal yields in AAA corporate paper remain meaningfully above inflation, offering a credible risk-adjusted return advantage over rolling FDs. However, three macro stresses materially complicate the near-term execution environment. First, the Hormuz disruption has driven Brent crude to $118.95/barrel (+86% YoY), a level that, if sustained, will transmit into Indian CPI through fuel and logistics channels, potentially pushing headline inflation toward or above the RBI's 4% target in H2 FY27 and constraining the rate-cut path that underpins the capital appreciation component of the bond fund thesis. Second, the INR has depreciated 10.7% over twelve months to 94.79 vs USD, with forex reserves declining $25 billion to $703 billion; imported inflation from this channel reinforces the crude-oil risk and may prompt RBI to maintain its pause longer than the market currently prices. Third, the 10Y G-Sec yield has risen 44 bps to 6.99% since November 2025 (spiking intraday to 7.19% on April 3), reflecting these pressures; any corporate bond fund with meaningful duration exposure faces mark-to-market headwinds if yields continue to rise, and the advisor should confirm the fund's modified duration and positioning before execution. The below-normal monsoon forecast (92% of LPA, 66% probability of below-normal or deficient outcome per IMD April 13) adds a further food-inflation tail risk that could materialise in Q3–Q4 FY27. On the structural side, the Indian Context module flags an unresolved product eligibility question under str_007: mutual fund debt products are not listed as confirmed eligible instruments for a private limited company investor structure, and this requires advisor resolution before commitment. The fiscal backdrop is constructive — consolidation on track, capex strong — but elevated government market borrowing (Rs 13.03L Cr in FY27) sustains supply pressure on yields. In aggregate, the macro environment does not invalidate the proposed action's underlying logic, but the combination of an active crude shock, INR stress, yield curve steepening, and an unresolved structural eligibility flag elevates the risk level to elevated and warrants advisor attention to duration positioning, execution timing relative to the September 2026 FD maturity, and resolution of the private limited company eligibility question before proceeding.",
  "data_points_cited": [
    "RBI repo rate: 5.25% as of April 17, 2026 (paused after 125 bps of cuts from 6.50%)",
    "Real interest rate: ~3.15% derived (repo 5.25% minus core CPI 2.1%, RBI MPC April 8, 2026)",
    "CPI headline: 3.40% YoY, March 2026 (rising from 3.21% February 2026)",
    "CPI core (ex-food, fuel, ex-precious metals): 2.1% Jan-Feb 2026 average (RBI MPC Statement April 8, 2026)",
    "CPI food: 3.87% YoY, March 2026",
    "10Y G-Sec yield: 6.99% as of April 17, 2026 (up from 6.55% November 2025; intraday spike to 7.19% on April 3)",
    "Brent crude: $118.95/barrel close April 29, 2026 (from ~$64 April 2025, +86% YoY; Hormuz disruption blocking ~20% global oil trade)",
    "USD/INR: 94.787 as of April 29, 2026 (from 85.60 April 2, 2025; 10.7% depreciation over 12 months)",
    "Forex reserves: $703B (down $25B from prior level)",
    "FII net equity flows April 2026: Rs -56,401 Cr (~$5.97B outflow); heaviest day April 2 at Rs -19,837 Cr",
    "DII net flows April 1-8, 2026: Rs +34,617 Cr (partial data)",
    "India-US rate differential: 162 bps (RBI repo 5.25% minus Fed midpoint 3.625%)",
    "US Fed funds rate: 3.50%-3.75%, hold as of March 18, 2026 FOMC",
    "GDP growth Q3 FY26: 7.8% YoY (Q1: 6.7%, Q2: 8.4%); RBI FY26 projection 7.6%",
    "PMI Manufacturing April flash: 55.9 (March final: 53.9); PMI Services April flash: 57.9; Composite PMI: 58.3",
    "Capacity utilisation Q3 FY26: 75.6% (above 75% late-expansion threshold)",
    "Non-food credit growth YoY: 14.3% as of February 28, 2026",
    "IMD monsoon forecast April 13, 2026: below normal, 92% of LPA; P(below normal + deficient) = 66%",
    "Fiscal deficit YTD Apr-Feb FY26: Rs 12,52,649 Cr (80.38% of RE), -7.0% YoY",
    "Government capex YTD: Rs 9,29,322 Cr (+14.46% YoY, 84.81% of RE)",
    "FY27 fiscal deficit target: 4.3% of GDP; market borrowing Rs 13.03L Cr (Budget February 2026)",
    "WALR on fresh loans: 8.28% as of December 2025 (109 bps transmission from repo; data ~120 days stale)",
    "str_007 flag: mutual_fund_debt not listed as confirmed eligible product for private_limited_company/resident investor structure"
  ]
}
```
